We’re gifted something as precious as life, and we squander it senselessly…endlessly.

Truthfully we’re given this gift for such a short amount of time. Nobody truly lives long enough to fully understand or appreciate how precious life is.

We live lives of regret, violence, animosity to the end. And for what?

We’re too intelligent to keep doing this, or ought to be, but we keep repeating the same cycles. For decades, centuries, and millenniums. 

A lot of us don’t have time to even think… life is swim or sink from the start. Survival mode. 

We don’t always have time to appreciate how crazy and miraculous it is to have these lives. How much we could collectively do with them, yet don’t.

And that’s a shame

How good was 2018 LeBron?

LeBron James really went into Oracle arena with 4 Cavs fans and dropped 51 on prime Draymond Green and Andre Iguodala, and won following JR Smith’s game winning rebound and putback off a George Hill missed free throw.

James was unfazed yet again by the hostile Oracle environment, which included broadcaster Mark Jackson stating on national television that he wanted to have sex with James’s wife.

James broke his wrist beating his Akron Hammer too vigorously in celebration that night, yet still averaged 34 points for the series.

A moment where the greatest player of all time—while faced against all time greats Stephen Curry, Kevin Durant, Kevon Looney, and Klay Thompson—reminded the world that there’s a level to greatness.

We were all witnesses 👑🐐

One of the coldest parts of Steph’s legacy is how his unprecedented dominance at 6’3” 38 years old is a result of an era he created 10 years ago.

It’s his game, his rules, and he’s the grandmaster of it.

NBA star Ja Morant may not have been placed in the best opportunity to play this regular season with the Memphis Grizzlies, but he and his team are putting themselves in the best positions to win off the court.

Morant and Catch12, his media and business venture company, have purchased a stake in the Levallois Metropolitans French basketball team, according to @TomerAzarly.

Over the last couple of years, the NBA and FIBA have quickly progressed towards a partnership to bring a new basketball league to Europe.

Morant and his team have been monitoring those conversations, and the minority ownership signals Morant’s interest building out his business portfolio outside of traditional means.

"I’m excited about how this first season with the Metros is going," Ja Morant told ClutchPoints. "They represent the culture, just like we do. I like what we are building and hopefully we get to take it into NBA Europe."

Boulogne-Levallois Metropolitans Basketball Club, the French Basketball team Ja Morant is investing in, is most recently and famously known for being the team that San Antonio Spurs star Victor Wembanyama played with before he made the jump to the NBA.

"We’re the fastest-growing sport right now in this country," NBA Commissioner Adam Silver said during the league’s two Europe games in January. "There’s, in essence, a golden era of the sport.

NBA Deputy Commissioner Mark Tatum has been tasked with leading the league's NBA Europe initiative, with significant interest from investors in 12 major European cities.

Paris is chief among them, which is why Morant and his team are capitalizing on the opportunity to own a team in Europe.

While it's still in its early stages, the target launch date for NBA Europe is sometime in 2027.

"You don't need to wait to win. You can do it right away. If you're that good, you're gonna make your team win regardless."

In the 2024 playoffs, Embiid played with Bell's palsy, which is a form of facial paralysis that causes blurred vision.

In the 2023 playoffs, Embiid missed a game in each of their two playoff series because of a sprained knee.

In the 2022 playoffs, Embiid suffered a concussion and a right orbital fracture. He also tore a ligament in his right thumb.

In 2021, Embiid missed only one game with a torn meniscus.

This year, Joel was averaging 26.9 PPG and 7.7 RPG before needing the appendectomy.
